TextENGNew: "...Still, driving around in a car with out-of-prefecture plates could put the villagers on edge.@k@vS11/11/420300152. I'd feel better if you rode with me in my car."
changeReason: New translation uses substantially different vocabulary and phrasing, resulting in a meaningfully different rendering of the Japanese text.

TextENGNew: ...Ooishi had a valid point. Tomoe reconsidered, admitting she had perhaps been a little careless.
If she was simply going to visit Reina Ryuugu, she could get by on her own... but there was still a chance some shadow was lurking behind the scenes.
In which case, it was likely best not to drive around in a car from another prefecture.
"...Understood.@k@vS11/28/420100639. Very well, I'll accept your hospitality."
Then, Tomoe was able to see various places within Hinamizawa while Ooishi was at the wheel.
Irie Clinic, the only hospital in the area.
The households of the Kimiyoshi and Sonozaki families, of the "Three Families."
The forestry service building currently being used as the Hinamizawa branch school...
And then...
"...Does Reina Ryuugu go to school here?"
"Yes, that's right.@k@vS11/11/420300155. Although today is a holiday, so there's nobody here."
From inside the car, she turned her head to look at the school building on the other end of the yard.
The heavy construction equipment and woodworking tools casually placed beside it made it look more like a logging facility than a school building.

"The school in Okinomiya is an extremely long distance from here. Not too far to walk if you had to, but still far.@k@vS11/11/420300157. So, the people in the village converted this to a school for the sake of the children."
"...However, at the time there was a completely different reason."
"They wanted to appeal to the mass media with a scandalous story about how the dam construction project was depriving the kids of their school."

...It was cunning, but effective. Even in Kakiuchi, when residents built their homes on the planned airport construction site, the people appealed to the mass media pleading for them not to destroy the source of the children's precious education.

The crops they went through painstaking effort to grow would be devastated, and the children were crying. ...Tomoe had a bitter smile face as she remembered watching the adults act out the farce on TV.
"Anyway, it was rather amazing.@k@vS11/11/420300161. No matter how you look at it, that's one thing you can say."
"Violence, stalking, underhanded deals... Their village was on the brink of being lost, so they had to put everything they had into fighting it, with no regard for the law."
"And their faith in Oyashiro-sama was a key element of that unification."
"...I see."
However, when Tomoe listened to Ooishi's explanation... something felt out of place.
Just like in the coffee shop earlier, Ooishi seemed to have a strong emotional stake in the dam construction project and the curse of Hinamizawa. Almost an obsession.

Even when discussing the various incidents so far, he attempted to think and analyze them with a certain degree of objectivity and wasn't one to get emotional when it came to an investigation, but here...
"By the way, how is Reina Ryuugu's school life?"
"Hmm, about that. We haven't received much information from the school, so the details are thin.@k@vS11/11/420300165. ...However, I think it's probably fairly ordinary."
"Actually, I haven't heard any talk about her causing problems. In fact, her younger classmates seem to have a good impression of her."
"...Then why was it that you considered looking into her a little while back, Ooishi-san?"
"It was related to Satoshi Houjou-kun's disappearance...@k@vS11/11/420300168. I heard that he spoke with her before he went missing."
"About "Oyashiro-sama.""
"...About "Oyashiro-sama?""
...Ooishi told Tomoe what he heard from another female student.
According to her, Reina Ryuugu apparently consulted with Satoshi Houjou a few days before this year's "Watanagashi" incident occurred.
"About frequently feeling like there was somebody following behind him...@k@vS11/11/420300171. And when he first asked, she acted like she already understood."
"Everything Satoshi-kun has been experiencing... is an omen of Oyashiro-sama's curse."
"...Someone is staring at you from a distance.@k@vS11/02/420700003. ...Someone is constantly following you.@k@vS11/02/420700004. ...Someone is always watching you from behind."
"...Eventually, you'll be able to hear an extra footstep that wasn't your own."
"At first, it'll only happen outside.@k@vS11/02/420700007. ...But soon enough, it'll be following behind you even in your own home."

"And... even after you turn off the lights and crawl into your futon... it'll be standing by your bedside, looking down on you.@k@vS11/02/420700009. ...In total silence. Patiently waiting...@k@vS11/02/420700010. Until you admit your sins."

"At first, Satoshi-kun mentioned that he was having bad dreams.@k@vS11/02/420700012. ...So, what kind of dreams were they? When I asked him about it... they reminded me of my own.@k@vS11/02/420700013. That's why I had to warn him."

"...Those signs would get closer and closer, until you could hear the footsteps... constantly looking down on Satoshi-kun.@k@vS11/02/420700015. ...It sounded just like my experience... so I had to talk to him about it."
"And that's... "Oyashiro-sama?""

"Right.@k@vS11/11/420300173. After that, Satoshi Houjou-kun was especially worried about his family problems, which we thought was his motivation for committing the murder.@k@vS11/11/420300174. He and his sister were living with their aunt and uncle, but they didn't get along."
"The boy's sister wasn't taking it very well. There were daily conflicts. It was a big fuss...@k@vS11/11/420300176. Protecting his sister from his aunt and uncle had to be painfully exhausting."
"Did the police not do anything to help...?"
"They couldn't do anything... Not without solid evidence.@k@vS11/11/420300178. The councilor's office said the same thing."
"Not to mention, there's the problem of how the village treated the Houjou family... The people of Hinamizawa pretended they didn't see anything."
Three years ago, Satoshi Houjou's parents went against the village and refused to oppose the Hinamizawa dam project. They even attempted to convince other villagers to support it.
As a result, they were branded as "traitors" who sold out their hometown... Even after Houjou and his wife died in the accident two years ago, nobody offered them any sympathy.
Not only that, but the villagers continued to treat their children coldly...
Two youngsters who couldn't find any relief for their unfair treatment... so they simply continued to endure it...
"...What an awful story."

"It's shameful, but we didn't have the power to help them.@k@vS11/11/420300181. After that, the surviving uncle became terrified of the curse and moved in with his mistress in Okinomiya. And the sister started living together with her friend."
"...The story is far from resolved, but I suppose you could say they did escape from a living hell."
She looked out the window while enduring the painful conversation. ...She couldn't simply see it as someone else's problem.
changeReason: New translation removes reference to tomoe, changing who is referenced in the scene.

TextENGNew: (No matter how much of a burden it was, there's no way she can be happy with her brother gone...)
Tomoe thought back on her own past.
When Madoka left and Tomoe was on her own... life certainly did get easier.

Her living expenses were cut in half, so she didn't have to work impossibly hard at her part-time job. And the time she spent visiting her at the hospital for rehabilitation could be freely used for her own purposes.
But... if anyone asked if she was happy, the answer would be no.
Her grandmother often went out of town on business trips, so even if she returned home nobody would be there.
She'd turn on the lights, leave the TV on even though she wasn't watching it... and make a meal for one, then eat it in silence.

Her excessively serious personality proved disastrous, as well. She couldn't call a friend to make small talk, and she couldn't play outside... She had an extremely boring college life of nothing but studying.
Back then, she was constantly haunted by thoughts about her sister's current well-being...
That loneliness prevented her from distracting herself with anything else.
(How is the boy's younger sister doing? I wonder if she feels the same way I did...?)
Perhaps she wasn't feeling as lonely, since she was living with a close friend.
Still, whenever she woke up in the middle of the night, or whenever she came home alone... she immediately remembered.
The pain of the absence of people whose presence you always took for granted...
"Anyway... Tomoe-chan?"
"Huh? Aah, sorry. We were talking about that story about Oyashiro-sama, right?@k@vS11/28/420100655. So, how does that tie back to Reina Ryuugu?"
"Apparently, Reina Ryuugu seems to think that the "curse of Oyashiro-sama" is responsible for her family problems and her parents' divorce."
"In fact, she made remarks seeming to confirm Oyashiro-sama's existence both before and after the incident.@k@vS11/11/420300186. ...Signs of someone following behind her, making footsteps."
"...What on earth could that be?"

"To be honest, I don't know.@k@vS11/11/420300188. It's too much to think it's just some delusion, and two different people who caused incidents both had similar experiences... but it can't be so simple as tying it back to superstition."
Tomoe stole a glance at Ooishi, who was looking in the direction they were traveling.
His expression implied he wasn't joking around, so she couldn't think of anything to say in response.
When she first agreed to Ooishi's request to help on an investigation, she didn't think it would be such a difficult story.
A girl under some sort of stress exploded one day and violently lashed out. ...That was the impression she got.
And, in order to evade her guilty conscience about the crime she committed, she subconsciously passed the responsibility onto something else she knew, and convinced herself it was the truth...
It was a common phenomenon for adolescent boys and girls, so she thought that Reina Ryuugu was going through something similar.
But... two people who seemed to have very little contact both described the same abnormal fear of this "Oyashiro-sama" before turning to violent behavior.
So there may be more to what they're saying than simply deflecting responsibility...
After that, Ooishi passed through the center of the village and headed into a mountainous area.
And the weeds growing along the side of the road as they arrived made it seem like there were no signs of human activity.
"...Where are we?"
"This is the site of the Hinamizawa dam war I told you about earlier.@k@vS11/11/420300190. The tasteless scene where warriors clashed."
Tomoe and Ooishi stepped out of the car and stood in front of the site.
It was a mountain of garbage, filled with scrap and bulky trash.
It was completely different from the scenic village. A sad place, almost like ruins.
Apparently the villagers rarely visited here, so the soil was abundantly filled with weeds.
The huge volume of garbage was probably dumped here illegally by people from nearby towns and villages.
After the people left things here, they remained here untouched and permanently exposed to the elements.
"...What an eyesore.@k@vS11/28/420100661. Has nobody taken any action about this social problem?"

"Well, there's a lot of surplus land in the mountains, and the procedures for government offices to deal with it are far too troublesome.@k@vS11/11/420300192. If you speak up about it, they'll just say, "Then why don't you do something about it?" so it'll have the opposite effect."
"...But the truth is, after putting on the charade of the dam war, their subsequent embarrassment led them to neglect the post-processing procedures. It's an absurd story."
"Charade... you say?"
Ooishi nodded and said "yeah" in response to Tomoe's question, then lit a cigarette.
...According to his story, the illegal dumping at this trash heap was connected to the dam opposition movement.
Since crooked businessmen were illegally dumping bulky trash and industrial waste at this site outside the village, they established a checkpoint to defend it.
...In fact, the problem of illegal dumping had apparently been going on for a long time, so they had a strong argument for it.
However... it was never about industrial trash. Rather, the main objective was to stop construction-related vehicles for inspection and delay the progress of the construction project.
"In fact, they lost a tremendous amount of time to reviews of official documents and verifying the quantity of cargo.@k@vS11/11/420300195. The on-site manager was constantly upset about it."

"Although the big-shot government officials tried to appeal to the residents in the opposition movement, they also placed a person in charge of the site with the responsibility of recovering construction delays, even if just by a single day.@k@vS11/11/420300197. ...When you drink, you let your emotions show."
"Ooishi-san, did you know this on-site manager?"
"Yeah, he was a very old friend.@k@vS11/11/420300199. Like an older brother, or a father... He was strict, but he was a good man with a warm heart.@k@vS11/11/420300200. I learned a lot from him."
"That's right, it's like your relationship with Yamaoki-san, Tomoe-chan. Or if it's not too presumptuous of me to say, like your relationship with me."
Just then... Tomoe suddenly remembered.
Ooishi told her about the "curse of Oyashiro-sama" murder from three years ago when they were at the coffee shop earlier.
That's right, the victim in that case was the on-site manager of the dam construction project, wasn't it...?!
"Ooishi-san. No way... that man was...?"

"Yeah, that's right.@k@vS11/11/420300203. ...He was killed somewhere in this mountain of garbage on the night of Watanagashi three years ago.@k@vS11/11/420300204. The corpse was dismembered... and one arm still hasn't been recovered."
"It can't be..."
She looked toward Ooishi, utterly at a loss for words. He smoked his cigarette while staring at the mountain of garbage... gazing at the former dam construction site.
changeReason: New translation removes reference to tomoe, changing who is referenced in the scene.

TextENGNew: "Year after year, I say that this is the year I'll catch the culprit.@k@vS11/11/420300206. And that the curse won't claim any more victims.@k@vS11/11/420300207. ...I swore that in front of his grave.@k@vS11/11/420300208. But in the end, it happened again this year."
"And it robbed the future of a poor boy.@k@vS11/11/420300210. ...Every time I think about it, my chest aches."
"Ooishi-san..."
"...@k@vS11/11/420300212.I admire you, Tomoe-chan.@k@vS11/11/420300213. Even after 11 years, you haven't lost your convictions."

"Even though it's only been three years in my case... sometimes my rage and hatred for the culprit drive me to the edge.@k@vS11/11/420300215. For you, it's been three or four times that.@k@vS11/11/420300216. ...There's no way I could bear it if I were in your position."
"Standing in your position has given me even more respect for you."
Tomoe closed her eyes and bit her lip.
If she had to challenge her parents' enemy completely on her own, how would she have managed?
Surely by now... she would've given in to despair navigating the labyrinth of the unknown, or continued pursuing it until it broke her.
Tomoe was able to keep working hard until today thanks to Ooishi and Yamaoki... and because she had Madoka.
But Ooishi was shouldering that burden on his own.
...There was no easy way to say which experience was more painfully difficult.
Anger... hatred... and revenge.
Constantly thinking those thoughts, and enduring the pain and suffering... over and over again.
I surely said it was everything to me.
I've said many times that it's not possible to move on until I overcome this.
But... sometimes, I get anxious.
I wonder if the world lying beyond this is really the world I want to see...
So... I understand. The pain bearing down on Ooishi's shoulders, and the weight of his sorrow...
"...Ooishi-san."
"Yes?"
"Umm... I understand that it's not really my position to say this, but even so."
"If there's anything I can do to help you, please tell me.@k@vS11/28/420100673. No matter what happens, I'll do everything in my power to help."
"...Tomoe-chan."
Ooishi stomped out his cigarette, then turned to Tomoe with a tranquil expression.
His eyes had a brilliant, clear shine to them that exemplified his strong will.
Ooishi gazed off somewhere, searching through his memories, then quickly arrived at one.
That was... the detective from the Metropolitan Police Department, who turned to him over 10 years ago with strong eyes as sharp as a knife.
"Let's go, Ooishi-san!" ...Even now, he couldn't forget the fearless smile on his face as he said that.
At the time, he certainly felt a sense of justice that he'd nearly forgotten.
Then, the sight of him rushing through a storm of bullets without hesitation, gun in hand, in order to protect a comrade...
Seeing the look in his eyes at the time... moved him to tears, for the first time in ages.
...She sure has grown stronger...
Ooishi remembered... the first time he met Tomoe.
Honestly, at that time... he was worried that she was a delicate girl who couldn't endure the tragedy of losing her family.
But... look at her now.
Her petite, unassuming body was stretched tall and full of vitality, and she even had the compassion to be concerned about him.
After 11 years... that image of a frail fledgling was completely gone.
Living such a long life... when Ooishi looked at her, he could feel the warmth from the bottom of his heart.
"Yes, thank you for the help.@k@vS11/11/420300221. Just hearing you say that makes me want to push myself even harder.@k@vS11/11/420300222. Nfu fu!"
"Ahahaha, you're exaggerating again.@k@vS11/28/420100675. Although, I don't really know how much help I can be from Kakiuchi, but...@k@vS11/28/420100676. ...Huh?"
"? What's wrong?"
"Is that... a girl?"
Ooishi looked in the direction Tomoe was pointing.

The view was partially hidden behind the shadow of the oversized trash... but it was just as Tomoe said. Someone with a girlish figure was looking down into the slope of the garbage mountain, and her hair swayed as she attempted to pull something out.
"What on earth is she doing in a place like this?"
"...Hmm.@k@vS11/11/420300225. That's probably the girl from this case, Reina Ryuugu."
"Huh? That's her?"
After hearing Ooishi's answer, She looked back at the girl once again.
changeReason: New translation removes reference to tomoe, changing who is referenced in the scene.

TextENGNew: She could tell it was a girl from her skirt-like clothing, but she was too far away to get a good look at her appearance.
"You can tell from this far away?@k@vS11/28/420100681. Could you see her face?"
"No, but there's no doubt about it.@k@vS11/11/420300227. No other girl would come to a place like this."
"I've heard from the residents and other children, she sometimes comes here to dig up trash on her days off and after school."
"Trash...? What for?"

"Well... it seems that she's hunting for treasure. When she finds something she likes, she takes it home with her.@k@vS11/11/420300230. You might say it's a hobby... but in part, you could call it eccentric behavior."
"Eccentric... behavior?"
It certainly was peculiar.
It was unusual for girls of her age to pick up trash, much less take it home...
Of course, there could still be usable tools and appliances in there, and it wasn't impossible to find cute things like stuffed animals.
However... a common thought among adolescents is an aversion to things they consider "unclean."
They wouldn't want to touch something dirty. But that wasn't all.
It's only a slight exaggeration to say they would declare something "unclean" and refuse to accept it as their own property because someone else had touched it or used it once.
Nevertheless, Reina Ryuugu enjoyed collecting garbage as a hobby, just as the villagers said...
What was the meaning behind that act?
Does she have some unique sense of value? Or something else...?
No... Tomoe had a memory of something she read in a book, long ago.
When people feel a sense of alienation or loneliness, they have a tendency to fill their living space with other people, animals, or "things" in general.
One theory suggested that behavior originated from the flocking instinct of animals.
They say humans are stressed by empty spaces and environments. They're always connected with something, so they can't feel mentally stable unless they feel something close to them.
So, even those living isolated from society will become agitated after just a few days if left alone in an empty space with nothing but food.
Decorating a room with flowers and pictures, or keeping an animal as a pet are typical examples of this phenomenon.
And, when that pressing mental condition is consistently nurtured... they say it can manifest as excessive "greed."
A wealthy person extravagantly spends their money like flowing water. This seemingly wasteful act is actually necessary to maintain their mental stability.
Additionally, even those without wealth will spend on debt after debt if suddenly given a lump sum... sometimes even leading down the road to ruin.

However, not wanting to waste things in their living environment... or when surrounded by people they don't like the character of, they can choose to "recklessly adopt animals," "decorate with excessive quantities of flowers"... or in some extreme cases, "bring home things abandoned by other people."
What if Reina Ryuugu's habit of taking trash home with her was a sign of mental instability...?
And what if there was some external factor that could've driven her into those circumstances...?
"Ooishi-san.@k@vS11/28/420100685. I'm sure you've heard that Reina Ryuugu's parents are divorced, right?"
"Yes, that's right.@k@vS11/11/420300232. And right after that, she caused the violent incident at school that you investigated, Tomoe-chan."
"Then, after receiving psychiatric counseling and being diagnosed with severe stress, both mental and physical, it was decided that moving to Hinamizawa would help her stabilize."
"...That's what the nurse at her previous hospital told me."
"So, did she receive some kind of medical treatment after coming to this village?"
"Medical... treatment?"
"Yeah.@k@vS11/28/420100688. At least, even if she transferred schools, she shouldn't be able to cease hospitalization so easily."
It was an offhanded question... but once Tomoe put it into words, she started feeling even more suspicious.
Even if the prosecution dropped the case against the girl who caused the incident, she would still be obligated to stay in long-term counseling for her mental instability.

Additionally, when relocation makes it impossible to continue using the same medical institution, it is common practice for a similar institution in the new area to inherit the medical records and treatment methods of the original institution.
"...Ooishi-san, after Reina Ryuugu transferred to Hinamizawa, didn't you go to the hospital in Ibaraki to ask about that?"
"Yes, of course.@k@vS11/11/420300237. So, if she did receive treatment, there is only one major hospital in Gogura with the equipment and personnel to help her..."
"If that's the case... couldn't we investigate to see what sort of condition she's in now?"
"Tomoe-chan... what do you mean by that?"

"Reina Ryuugu still hasn't recovered from her illness.@k@vS11/28/420100692. ...Or rather, in a sense it's getting worse.@k@vS11/28/420100693. And there's a chance her abnormal behavior might be related to this "Oyashiro-sama" you mentioned earlier, Ooishi-san."
Tomoe gazed at Reina Ryuugu as she attempted to dig something out of the mountain of trash, then turned back toward Ooishi.

"Satoshi Houjou was involved with Oyashiro-sama's curse... So in other words, there's a high likelihood that it's also connected to this year's murder case, like you mentioned in our earlier conversation."

"And it's possible that Satoshi Houjou was also suffering from a disease with the same symptoms as her.@k@vS11/28/420100696. Because they both had the same fear of "Oyashiro-sama" before their respective incidents."
"...And so, by grasping her mental state and understanding the people involved, maybe we can find some clue to help us understand the thought process that drove Satoshi Houjou to commit murder?"
"Hmm... So, Tomoe-chan, do you think Reina Ryuugu might be involved in this murder case?"

"Incidentally, the victim Tamae Houjou was killed during the Watanagashi festival, but several people witnessed Reina Ryuugu at the festival grounds at the time of the incident.@k@vS11/11/420300241. It's a rock solid alibi."
"No, Reina Ryuugu probably has nothing to do with that case."
"However, if her incident in Ibaraki was caused by someone else's intention just as you said,@k@vS11/28/420100700. ...it's not strange to think there's a common element behind both of them."
"If we can figure out what that is, maybe we can see who's pulling the strings behind the series of mysterious deaths in Hinamizawa?"
"Hmm... you're certainly not without persuasive power, but that's some bold reasoning."
Ooishi's thoughts stirred around Tomoe's suggestion.
About mental illness and using "faith in Oyashiro-sama" to control the village.

It was as if a completely unrelated story, but if Satoshi Houjou suffered from some mental illness that led to the crime, then there was a chance that the mastermind behind the incident was utilizing that mental illness.
"I know it's an unreasonable argument.@k@vS11/28/420100703. But the two of them both used the key word "Oyashiro-sama" before their incidents, and I can't help but focus on that."
"If we keep scrubbing at it, we'll be able to see something we couldn't see before... That's how I feel."

"...I see.@k@vS11/11/420300244. At the moment, we don't have enough information about Reina Ryuugu to assess her properly.@k@vS11/11/420300245. But the truth is, we're blocked on all sides.@k@vS11/11/420300246. So let's try taking your approach, Tomoe-chan."
With that, Ooishi tossed his cigarette to the ground and stomped it out. Then, he glanced at his watch.
"Anyway, I think it's about time we head out.@k@vS11/11/420300248. I'm sorry for dragging you to somewhere unnecessary."
"No, this was helpful.@k@vS11/28/420100706. ...I was originally planning to visit Reina Ryuugu's father after this. Would that be all right?"
"Yes, of course. I'll lead the way...@k@vS11/11/420300250. Nfu fu."
Then, Ooishi headed toward the parked car while twirling the key around his fingertip. Tomoe attempted to follow behind him... but she stumbled a bit on a stone and lost her footing.
"Whoops. ...Huh?"
That moment.
She suddenly looked back toward the mountain of garbage... and gasped.
changeReason: New translation removes reference to tomoe, changing who is referenced in the scene.

TextENGNew: Off in the edge of her line of sight, there was a girl far enough away to look the size of her little finger.
Standing there quietly... staring at Tomoe without moving a muscle.
She couldn't quite make out the girl's expression.
However.
She could clearly feel the cold hostility dwelling in her eyes.
...Who are you?
She shook her head to dismiss the voice in her imagination.
There was no way she could hear a voice from this distance. It was difficult enough just to discern her appearance.
She turned and began walking toward the car while those thoughts nagged at her.
changeReason: New translation removes reference to tomoe, changing who is referenced in the scene.

TextENGNew: Nevertheless, she felt it.
It felt like a sharp gaze was burning through her back, and it gave her a chill.
Heading back from the garbage mountain, a white building came into view as they approached the center of the village.
Ooishi went on to explain that it was the only little hospital in the village... the Irie Clinic.
"Oh? What's that...?"
A black car was parked by the front door of the Irie Clinic, and a young man in a white coat was talking to another man dressed in a suit.
Then, when the man noticed Ooishi driving the car, he rushed over to the driver's seat.
"Aah, Ooishi-san! You're in just the right place!@k@vS11/19/VT3B_kumaga2002. Nice timing."
"Kuma-chan? What are you doing here...?"

"Well, you said you were going to Hinamizawa, Ooishi-san. So I guessed that you were going to the Irie Clinic.@k@vS11/19/VT3B_kumaga2004. I couldn't reach you on the police radio, so I came here directly to meet you."
"The radio? That's weird..."
As soon as he heard that, Ooishi fiddled with the radio beside the steering wheel.
Indeed, the light was out and there was no indication it was receiving transmissions.
"Oh, it really isn't working. What a bother.@k@vS11/11/420300255. ...So, did you need something?"

"Huh? Did you forget?@k@vS11/19/VT3B_kumaga2006. Today is the day of the get-together meeting, isn't it?@k@vS11/19/VT3B_kumaga2007. But the chief was making a fuss since the meeting organizer wasn't in, so I came here in a hurry."
"Meeting organizer...? Who's that?"
"It's you, Ooishi-san."
As soon as he said that, Ooishi stopped to think for a while, before shouting "ohhh" and smacking his forehead.
"Right, right... Today is Monday, isn't it?@k@vS11/11/420300258. Thanks to the holiday, it still felt like Sunday."
"Good grief...@k@vS11/19/VT3B_kumaga2010. So, who is this?"
"Ah, this is our first time meeting.@k@vS11/28/420100711. My name is Minai, I'm the Chief Inspector from Kakiuchi Station."
As She stepped out of the car, she bowed her head to the man Ooishi called Kuma-chan. Ooishi went on to introduce the man as a subordinate of his, Detective Kumagai.
changeReason: New translation removes reference to tomoe, changing who is referenced in the scene.

TextENGNew: "So, what are you going to do, Ooishi-san?@k@vS11/19/VT3B_kumaga2012. If we leave now, we can still make it in time."
"Hmm, that's true, but I also made a promise to Tomoe-chan. Hmmm..."
"Oh, if that's the case then please go, Ooishi-san.@k@vS11/28/420100713. I'll go on alone."
"Oh dear, I can't do that.@k@vS11/11/420300261. ...Do you know how to get to the Ryuugu residence?"
"Ah, no.@k@vS11/28/420100715. But based on the drive up here, this seems like a fairly wide area.@k@vS11/28/420100716. I think I'll be all right with a map and some notes."
"Well, this is definitely not the place to get lost..."
Ooishi crossed his arms and groaned, still trying to decide.
(I appreciate his concern, but still...)
Tomoe bitterly smiled. Apparently Ooishi still had a strong image of her from the old days, and was treating her like a child.

She certainly took heed to Ooishi's words in the beginning and paid careful attention to her surroundings, but none of the villagers out and about seemed at all concerned. ...Well, it'll probably be fine.
...His defensive posture seemed a little excessive.

"...Man, you're such a worrywart, Ooishi-san.@k@vS11/28/420100719. Maybe I don't look like it, but I'm an adult, you know?@k@vS11/28/420100720. And we haven't run into any problems since coming here, have we?"
"...Well, I suppose that's true.@k@vS11/11/420300264. In that case, I must apologize for leaving you behind.@k@vS11/11/420300265. ...Oh, will you be all right with the car?"
"Yeah, although it might be a bit of a problem that I can't use the radio.@k@vS11/28/420100722. ...And sorry to be a bother, but do you have a map I could use?"
"Got it. Just a moment."
And then, Ooishi tore a page out of his police notebook and quickly drew a map, then handed it to Tomoe.
...The text was a bit hard to read, but it was easy to understand because he marked every street corner.
"Tomoe-chan."
"Yes?"

"For now, let me just say this in the interest of caution.@k@vS11/11/420300269. ...Please come straight back to Okinomiya as soon as you're done visiting the Ryuugu household.@k@vS11/11/420300270. Can you promise me that?"
"Ooishi-san, you're overreacti...?"
Ooishi... wasn't smiling.
He stared at Tomoe with a solemn expression as he handed her the key.
It felt like he was a little too worried about the situation, but she appreciated his concern, so she nodded.
"...Understood.@k@vS11/28/420100726. I'll head straight back after I meet the father and hear his story."
"I'll head back to Okinomiya Station as soon as I can to wait for you.@k@vS11/11/420300272. ...If you need anything, please don't hesitate to contact me."
"Got it.@k@vS11/28/420100728. Thanks for letting me borrow this car."
With that, Tomoe took the key from Ooishi, climbed into the car, and started the engine.
...Ooishi watched through the rear-view mirror until Tomoe's car disappeared around the corner.
Tomoe drove toward the Ryuugu household, following the notes Ooishi provided her.
She found the house with less trouble than expected.
"Is this it...?"
She stepped out of the car, closed the door, and subtly looked over her surroundings. ...There were no particular signs of other people around.
Then she stood in front of the entrance, drew a deep breath, and lightly knocked on the door.
"...Excuse me.@k@vS11/28/420100731. Is Ryuugu-san here?"
However, there was no response from inside. She knocked once more, this time a little louder, but she still couldn't hear anything from inside.
"May I come in?"
Still no reply.
"Not home, huh? ...Hmm?"
Suddenly feeling something out of place near her feet, she dropped her eyes and noticed something like blue paper near the tips of her shoes. When she picked it up, it was a small box of matches.
"Blue Mermaid, huh...?"
Tomoe was able to pull the name out of short term memory after seeing the flashy logo. There certainly was a flashy cabaret club named "Blue Mermaid" in one particular corner of the downtown area.
Naturally, that store was no place for a minor. So, it was likely dropped by Reina Ryuugu's father.
"That's right, I heard the father was unemployed..."
If so, he was in a good position. Tomoe had a prejudice against people who would spend time at bars downtown without having a regular job.

According to earlier investigation, he apparently received considerable compensation from his wife at the time of their divorce. But is there any chance that's the cause of his daughter's problems...?
Tomoe's eyes turned to a strange object in the yard when she softly stepped on it. It was some sort of mascot doll from a store, weathered by the elements.
And looking more closely... there were various dolls, stuffed animals, and things resembling machine parts sat down beside it.
It looked like they were carelessly sat down, rather than deliberately positioned.
"What on earth is she doing with that mountain of garbage...?"
She did speculate earlier that an unstable mental state could be leading her to this act of "garbage collection"... and her remarks about "Oyashiro-sama."
After all, Reina Ryuugu's mental state could be the reason for her unusual actions. ...And that theory was getting stronger and stronger.
That moment...
Tomoe suddenly felt something behind her and turned back.
...She couldn't see anything there. However,.
"Wh-what...? What the...?!"
She could hear a sound intermittently... and when she realized it was the sound of footsteps, a chill ran through her whole body.
"Everything Satoshi-kun has been experiencing... is an omen of Oyashiro-sama's curse."
"...Someone is staring at you from a distance.@k@vS11/02/420700019. ...Someone is constantly following you.@k@vS11/02/420700020. ...Someone is always watching you from behind."
"...Eventually, you'll be able to hear an extra footstep that wasn't your own."
Remembering Ooishi's earlier story about Reina Ryuugu... She suddenly turned pale and swallowed her spit.
changeReason: New translation removes reference to tomoe, changing who is referenced in the scene.

TextENGNew: ...What am I afraid of?
Even after hearing that story, isn't it unreasonable to get this nervous...?
In which case, how do you explain that sound just now...?
She tried many times to shake it off as just her imagination... but every single time.
*pat* *pat* *pat* *pat*.
*tip* *tip* *tip* *tip*.
The footsteps immediately negated that denial... as if to mock her.
*pat* *pat* *pat* *pat*.
*tip* *tip* *tip* *tip*.
Even after surveying the surrounding area... nobody was there.
There was... nothing suspicious at all.
What is the meaning of this?
Could this be what Reina Ryuugu was referring to? "Oyashiro-sama's"... curse...?!
Did she anger the gods by snooping around in a village where she didn't belong...?
"...Ah, ahahaha.@k@vS11/28/420100742. There's no such thing..."
Tomoe forced a laugh while struggling to deny the story she heard earlier.
Impossible.
That's right, it's impossible.
Someone, or some ghost-like entity... was definitely following her...!!
Even so, Tomoe couldn't cast aside the creepy sensations plaguing her heart, so she left the Ryuugu household in a hurry.
Then, she opened the door to Ooishi's car, jumped inside, and turned the key.
However, the engine wouldn't start. It simply continued making idle sounds.
Finding that suspicious, Tomoe opened the door... and at that point, she suddenly noticed the pungent smell of gasoline.
"What's this...?"
She stepped out of the car and stood beside it, searching for the source of the odor.
changeReason: New translation removes reference to tomoe, changing who is referenced in the scene.

TextENGNew: It was the gas cap in the back of the car.
It was smashed open... and there was a fuel hose for a kerosene stove or something laying on the ground below, covered in gasoline.
"...No way."
...That couldn't be the work of a ghost.
But did that mean there was a person with malicious intentions nearby...?!
She fought hard to suppress the urge to panic.
Someone must've drained the fuel tank using this hose and spilled it on the ground. ...Technically, doing so wasn't impossible.
Still, she was only away from the car for a few minutes.
Not only that, but even though Tomoe went into the Ryuugu family's yard, she wasn't all that far away.
Could some suspicious individual have skillfully carried that act out while remaining completely unnoticed...?!
She looked left and right, then behind her, searching for signs of another person.
changeReason: New translation removes reference to tomoe, changing who is referenced in the scene.

TextENGNew: ...Nothing.
Nobody... was there?
Impossible.
That couldn't be.
Someone was able to break the gas cap, drain the gas from the car, and then disappear in such a brief time frame...?!
That couldn't be the work of humans.
Then, was it the work of the gods or something supernatural...?!
"...It can't be...!!"
Tomoe violently shook her head, then locked the car and set off back the way she came.
If her memory was correct, there should be a phone booth just a short distance away.
If she could make it there and contact Ooishi, or someone...
Every time she took a step.
She could hear the peculiar sound of a footstep just a moment later.
...It had to be an auditory hallucination.
She must just be tired.
After hearing so many strange and unsettling stories, her emotions were getting carried away...
Tomoe told herself that over and over along the way.
But... even so.
Up until now, she wasn't afraid of ghosts. She didn't even believe they existed.
But for the first time in her life... she felt a fear that froze her entire body.
She arrived at a fork in the road with a telephone booth right as it got completely dark out.
"Thank goodness... I made it in time."
Tomoe wiped the sweat from her forehead with her sleeve as she rushed into the telephone booth.
The road had very few streetlights, so if she had arrived just a little later she might've stumbled on her own feet in the dark.
She did bring a flashlight and kept it in the car, just in case... but unfortunately, she forgot to take it with her.
(I'm getting flustered...)
Tomoe muttered her bitter thoughts as she picked up the receiver.
However... it was also true that she didn't want to stay in that place for even a moment longer.
Was it less dread and more of a general agitation and feeling of danger?
Tomoe inserted three 10 yen coins, then opened her police notebook and dialed the number for Okinomiya Station.
After a few rings, she heard the voice of an older man saying, "...Hello."
"Ah, excuse me.@k@vS11/28/420100754. This is Minai from Kakiuchi Station. Detective Ooishi and I..."
"Ohh, is that Tomoe-chan?@k@vS11/11/420300274. It's me, Ooishi. Nfu fu!"
"Ooishi-san...? Why?"

"Sorry, you were taking a long time to get back, so I was feeling worried and decided to wait at the information desk for you to make contact.@k@vS11/11/420300276. Whew~, I'm relieved you finally called.@k@vS11/11/420300277. Where are you now?"
"Ah, right.@k@vS11/28/420100757. I'm at a phone booth in Hinamizawa village..."
Tomoe briefly explained the story of the gas being drained from the car. When Ooishi heard that, he responded "Understood" in a low voice that indicated he fully grasped the situation.
"I'll head out to meet you, right away.@k@vS11/11/420300279. Can you wait for about 10 minutes?"
"I'm sorry, thanks for the help.@k@vS11/28/420100759. Then I'll be waiting here."
"It's a bit dangerous to walk around, so please do stay there.@k@vS11/11/420300281. ...See you soon."
Ooishi hung up the phone after saying that.
"...Haah."
She let out a heavy sigh of relief as she sat the phone on the hook.
...She already knew that. She wasn't some child afraid of nonexistent things in the dark.
But the situation had gotten heavy enough that Tomoe wasn't able to walk any more.
"I'm sure Ooishi and the others are going to tease me about this for a while..."
Although... Tomoe didn't have the greatest eyesight in the dark, so walking at night was a bad choice anyway. It was best to avoid getting hurt by tripping and falling into a ditch.
(Anyway...)
Tomoe couldn't help but grumble to herself.
Regrettably, her long time living in the city had made her forget how dark and gloomy a country road at night could be.
It wasn't so bad in Ibaraki, although she did have to carry a flashlight at night.
"I've gotten soft..."
Once again, she let out a sigh.
...The telephone pole she could see through the glass was dimly lit and making an irritating sound.
There were countless shadows darting around from moths, or some other type of insect.
As soon as they approached the round light bulb, they were either blown away by the heat, or burned and gently fell to the ground.
(Rushing to their own demise...)
She couldn't help but shake her head. ...As she thought that, Tomoe pushed the door open, trying to get out of the enclosed space and get some fresh air.
That moment... Tomoe couldn't help but lightly raise her voice, and immediately jump back to steel herself.
Ahead of her... was a girl, expressionlessly staring at her with icy cold eyes...!
"Wh-who are you...?!"
She hadn't gotten a good look at her face.
She'd only been able to make out her hairstyle and clothes from far away.
However.
Tomoe clearly realized... that the girl in front of her was Reina Ryuugu.
"D-do you need something from m...?"
"...Do you need something from me?"
Tomoe's words were cut off... by Reina Ryuugu, striking back at her with the same exact question, and still no expression on her face.
The coldness in her tone... was as sharp as a knife.
The two eyes staring straight at her had a sinister glow amongst the darkness.
A stiff chill coursed through Tomoe's back.
Without her even realizing it, the girl had already trapped her within that narrow box.
Reina slowly stepped into the box to squeeze her even tighter.
And then, she noticed Tomoe's police notebook... and stared at Tomoe with all her strength.
"...A detective, huh?"
"...You're a detective, aren't you?"
"Th... that's right."
"...Then, you already know, right?"
"You already know who I am, right?!"
"Guh?!"
Tomoe felt her neck being tightened as Reina stood right in front of her staring her down.
It happened so suddenly, Tomoe was unable to react.
"R-Ryuugu-sa...!!"
"Ahahahahahahahaha.@k@vS11/02/420700036. ...Die, you idiot.@k@vS11/02/420700037. Ahahahahahahaha, ahahahahahahahahahahahah!!!"
The scene before her eyes gradually grew darker.
Her forehead was in pain... and it felt cold and heavy inside her head.
She repeatedly opened and closed her mouth, gasping... but not a sound came out. Even breathing... was impossible.
As hard as she attempted to tear the hands away from her neck, they remained tight.
In fact, the grip grew even stronger... and the nails even started biting into her throat.
She could hear... something... A sound from far away.
No... This is... someone's... voice?
...What...? What are you...?!
And then,.
Tomoe lost consciousness.
"Ahahahahahahaha, ahahahahahahahahahahahah!!!"
